title: uapaca kirkiana var. benguelensis
intro: Uapaca kirkiana var. benguelensis is a variety of the Uapaca kirkiana tree, a species native to southern Africa, commonly known as the mutemwa or n'janssa tree. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Uapaca kirkiana var. benguelensis include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, among others. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, often made from the bark, roots, or leaves of the tree.
